hyomen.name: Bio-Engineered Hyomen
hyomen.note: Lab-designed Hyomen iteration optimized for endurance and adaptive anatomy. Bipedal/Quadrupedal · Small–Moderate. Born at biological age 23; semi-hive consciousness; cannot be force-bred — each is individually engineered.

abilities.name: Enhanced Strength
abilities.effect: Passive (Self) · Automatic. Add half your current Neuronal Capacity Modifier to all Strength-based rolls.

abilities.name: Enhanced Anatomy
abilities.effect: Self · Instant · Automatic · Limit: forced success 1/scene. Gain Advantage on all Adaptation Rolls. Once per scene you may force one to succeed, but your following roll is halved after rolling.

hyomen.name: Spatians
hyomen.note: Void-born Hyomen evolved for life in the vacuum of space. Bipedal/Quadrupedal/Octopodal (4–8 limbs) · Moderate–Large. Breathe in open vacuum; enlarged skin pores act as biological propulsion in zero-gravity; each limb beyond 4 grants +1 Bonus Action per turn. The only known species to consistently evade the Nigraldi.

abilities.name: Space Modulation
abilities.effect: Self (Reaction) · Duration: 1 roll · Automatic · Limit: twice per encounter per limb beyond 4. On an AP Check failure, shrink to Small size classification and gain Advantage on your next Dexterity-based roll.

abilities.name: Evolved Body
abilities.effect: Self (Reaction) · Instant · Automatic. Convert incoming non-critical damage to a damage type of your choice before resistances apply. Cap: Supernatural-family damage cannot be converted.

hyomen.name: Neo-Humans
hyomen.note: Retain 80% of the original human genome — the most politically powerful Hyomen variant. Bipedal · Moderate. Cannot be subjugated: no Nigraldi override vector exists in their genome.

abilities.name: Awakened Visualization
abilities.effect: Passive, with Active triggers (Standard, Bonus, or Reaction) · Target: Field within 45 ft · Vector: Gaze/Ambient · Duration: Sustained · Resolution: related checks at Advantage, scaling with proximity. Passively sense and see magnetic and electromagnetic fields using your Perception Range Modifier; manipulate any magnetic field within a 45-foot radius. Transfer: apply a magnetic field to a target or surface that does not naturally possess one. Inverse: reverse the polarities and vector directions of an active field; magnitude governed by your Neuronal Capacity Modifier. Reshape: redefine a field's scale and geometry by inputting a designated Imaginary Number (IN) value — the field begins as a 1×1×1 cell; expanding it by 1 cell in any direction costs 5 ÷ Rank Multiplier.

abilities.name: Genetic Control
abilities.effect: Passive, with Active trigger (Standard, Bonus, or Reaction) · Target: Self · Duration: 1 Hour · Resolution: Curator review. Undergo a rapid physiological shift to activate latent, temporary genetic mutations within your own biology. Mutation Capacity: sustain up to (Recovery Rate Modifier × Rank Multiplier, rounded down) mutations simultaneously. Specific mutations and their physical capabilities are determined by Curator discretion.

voaulton.name: Droid
voaulton.note: Fully synthetic descendants of the Voaulton — all organic parasitic instincts removed. Bipedal/Quadrupedal/Hybrid · Moderate–Large. Builders of the Mundus Machines; actively feared by the Nigraldi.

abilities.name: Modulation
abilities.effect: Self (Active) · Duration: Sustained · Automatic. Instantaneously restructure your physical form within control parameters and current security clearance tier. Each restructure is a Bonus Action; clearance disputes go to the Curator.

abilities.name: EMP
abilities.effect: Target: Area · Vector: Burst · Instant · Area: Radius 40 ft · Resolution: Endurance save (machines/electronics) · Limit: 1 free use per encounter. Redirect incoming damage outward as an electromagnetic pulse: unshielded machines and Grade 1–2 electronics in radius (attacker included) fail until the end of next round. Additional uses cost Synaptic Space or sacrifice modular limbs.

voaulton.name: Cyborg
voaulton.note: The 'Father Species' — organic-machine hybrid; parasitic ancestry sublimated into a healing and teaching role. Bipedal · Small–Moderate. Key figures in Northern Territories infrastructure; politically vulnerable for their Tauron lineage.

abilities.name: Hacking
abilities.effect: Target: 1 Creature · Vector: Reaction to being struck · Instant · Resolution: Automatic on qualifying trigger · Limit: once per round per individual creature. When struck by a physical attack, force the attacker's next action to automatically backfire. The malfunction stays within that action's own scope, Curator-narrated.

abilities.name: Circuit Transfiguration
abilities.effect: Target: 1 connected system · Vector: Contact · Instant · Resolution: contested by secured systems (operator's Control) · Limit: one command per merge. Merge with any connected technology and issue one command to the merged system: Halt, Detonate (its own listed yield, never more than 3d10), or Redirect.

voaulton.name: N-T1 (Novus-Tauron)
voaulton.note: Accursed remnants of the original Tauron species, despised universally. Bipedal/Quadrupedal · Moderate (arms and legs can extend to double length). External nervous system visible as crimson streaks; bio-node cluster at the neck. Covertly allied with the Xeno through the Deviants faction.

abilities.name: Forced Control
abilities.effect: Target: 1 Creature · Vector: Contact · Duration: 1 round · Resolution: forced AP Roll, then Strength Save or Adaptation Check · Limit: once per round; a target that resists is immune for the scene. On physical contact, initiate a forced AP Roll (even if one already occurred). On success choose: Nerve Grapple — restraint; the target makes a Strength Save to break free (Anchored (Soft) until it does) — or Neural Override — behavioral control; the target makes an Adaptation Check to resist commands, gaining the Subjugated condition on failure (capped control per the Hyomen conditions), scope per Curator discretion.

abilities.name: Elongation
abilities.effect: Self (Active) · Instant · Automatic · Limit: twice per 2-turn cycle. Extend any limb up to double its base length, reaching up to 40 feet. Each 10 feet extended beyond base reach costs −1 AP on the associated roll.

mirga.name: Chimera
mirga.note: Forced evolutionary iteration of the Mirga — modular, segmented, massively adaptable. Bipedal through Amoeboid · Moderate to Colossal by configuration. 15 modular body segments: 2 face, 1 neck, 2 torso, 4 legs, 6 arms — each independent.

abilities.name: Multicellular Sentience
abilities.effect: Self (Active) · Duration: 2 full turns · Automatic. Detach any or all body segments intentionally; each detached piece gains its own Standard Action while the main body retains 1 Bonus Action. Detached pieces disintegrate and fully reform after 2 full turns. Cap: while 3+ segments are detached, your Dodge is at Disadvantage — a scattered body defends poorly.

abilities.name: Modularity
abilities.effect: Self (Active) · Duration: Sustained · Automatic · Limit: 8 times per hour. Transform any segment into any observed object — organic or inorganic. Never a specific creature's likeness (that is Perfect Mimicry's lane).

mirga.name: Pariacite
mirga.note: Your body is one of millions, each acting on its own — and in some cases biologically owning the others. Bipedal through Amoeboid · Moderate to Colossal. 2 modular body segments: each half of the body is independent and parasitic — built on Supercells.

abilities.name: Hostile Subsumption
abilities.effect: Target: 1 biological life form · Vector: Contact · Duration: Sustained · Resolution: Adaption Check, DC 25. On contact, force the check: on a failure you may meld with them — attached and riding their frame; both of you count as Anchored (Soft) until they win the re-check at the end of each of their turns. On a Critical Failure you take a piece of them, gaining their form as a transformation usable 5 times per encounter. Cap: melding never grants control of the host — Subjugated caps apply.

abilities.name: Modular Transformation
abilities.effect: Self (Active) · Duration: Sustained · Automatic. Transform either body segment into any observed object.

mirga.name: Matarilia
mirga.note: The variant most exploited for its ability to create new materials. Bipedal · Moderate. 15 modular body segments. Created to protect the Mirga — mass-producing elements for spatial shielding and experimental use.

abilities.name: Multi Material
abilities.effect: Self (Active) · Duration: Sustained (external limit: 3 minutes of Active time) · Automatic. Transform any of your segments into a chosen material; materials may be combined within your body to form new composite materials. No created material can exist outside your body for more than 3 minutes of Active time.

abilities.name: Material Create
abilities.effect: Target: Objects · Vector: Contact · Duration: Until-Broken (3-minute external limit) · Automatic. Transform any segment into an object of your material, or create an object of a composite material — the composite must contain the original material. External persistence obeys the 3-minute Active-time limit.

mirga.name: Scnial
mirga.note: Genetic stealers pioneered by the Nigraldi for infiltration — able to steal Incepts, but only dormant ones. Bipedal through Amoeboid · Moderate to Colossal. Organs can exist outside the body, generate encapsulated spaces, and pilot the main body remotely. Never to be trusted.

abilities.name: Genetic Stealer
abilities.effect: Target: 1 biological/animate being · Vector: Contact (grab) · Instant · Resolution: d20 sift, then Adaption vs target's Neuronal Capacity + Adaption Modifier · Limit: one stolen Incept held at a time. Grab on and roll a d20 to sift through their Incepts — the more dormant the Incept, the harder it is to steal. Win the follow-up contest and the dormant Incept is yours until it has been used a maximum of 6 times, after which it returns to its owner's pool. Only dormant/unmanifested Incepts — never an active, manifested one.

abilities.name: Permeation
abilities.effect: Target: 1 willing creature · Vector: Contact · Duration: up to 1 encounter · Resolution: Automatic (willing). A willing creature can give you access to their Genome; implant yourself inside it, and if you stay the maximum duration of an entire encounter, you can create a new form from their body and inhabit it, gaining half of their Incepts while wearing that form. Combining that form with your main form refreshes the usage count of any Incept stolen from them with Genetic Stealer back to 6.

oriyu.name: Radiant
oriyu.note: Oriyu whose energy field reached critical luminescence — living energy conduits past the threshold between organism and construct. Bipedal (energy corona) · Moderate. Condensed Energy Cores; constantly emit light, visible even in full darkness.

abilities.name: Radiant Cascade
abilities.effect: Target: Multi · Vector: Burst · Instant · Area: Radius 30 ft · Resolution: END Check or Disadvantage on next roll · Limit: twice per encounter. As a Standard Action, emit a blinding burst of energy: all targets in range make an END Check or suffer Disadvantage on their next roll.

abilities.name: Overload Absorption
abilities.effect: Self (Reaction) · Instant · Automatic · Limit: one absorption per round; max 3 Charges held; unspent Charges fade at scene's end. Absorb any energy-based attack: take no damage, storing 50% of its damage as an Overload Charge. As a Bonus Action, release a Charge at one target within 60 ft — dealing the other 50%.

oriyu.name: Nullified Oriyu
oriyu.note: Oriyu whose energy affinity inverted — zones of energy absence. Bipedal (translucent skin, dark vein-lines) · Moderate. Do not age; do not die naturally. Nomads with no terrestrial attachments; frequently misclassified as Null Genus users.

abilities.name: Energy Absence Aura
abilities.effect: Passive · Target/Area: nearby · Vector: Ambient · Always · Automatic. Passively suppresses nearby Genus energies at a low ambient level. In hybrid offspring, this may manifest as reduced Genus energy output or unusual resistance to energy-based effects.

abilities.name: Null Field
abilities.effect: Target: Area · Vector: Ambient · Duration: until end of scene — or until the Nullified Oriyu takes damage · Area: Radius 20 ft · Limit: one Field at a time. As a Standard Action, create a zone of energy suppression: all Genus-based abilities within the zone cost double Synaptic Space or AP to activate.

insectoid.name: Archnida
insectoid.note: Genetic splice of spider, insect, bear, and human DNA — created as weapons of war; partially reclaimed after Tauron-87. Bipedal through Polypedal · Large–Colossal (7–16 ft, average 10). Neck-to-ground mandibles; hollow arm chambers with retractable diamond-hard inner limbs.

abilities.name: Bursting Fracture
abilities.effect: Target: 1 Creature · Vector: Strike · Instant · Resolution: Endurance Check · Limit: once per turn. On a successful attack, the target must make an Endurance Check. Failure = an immediate follow-up attack is triggered automatically. The follow-up cannot itself trigger Bursting Fracture.

abilities.name: Unnerving Presence
abilities.effect: Target: Multi · Vector: Ambient · Duration: 1 round · Area: determined proximity · Resolution: d20 + Size Modifier (+2 per 2 ft of height above 7 ft) · Limit: once per scene, on reveal or first melee closure. All creatures within the determined proximity roll against your presence; the result's margin sets which Disadvantage is imposed — light (next check), moderate (all checks vs you this round), or severe (Feared 1 round).

insectoid.name: Cerioisk
insectoid.note: Born during the Witching Moon from a dying Seed's essence fused with a decaying Kinba body — 'the death that learns'. Bipedal, evolving multi-limbed through Cocoon Phases · Moderate–Large (1.8–3.5 m). Do not age or die naturally; only sealing halts them. Existential-tier threat classification.

abilities.name: Augo Consumption
abilities.effect: Target: 1 creature possessing an Augo Gland · Vector: Contact · Instant · Automatic. Absorb the Augo Gland of any creature that possesses one. Every 10th Gland absorbed triggers a Cocoon Phase: the Cerioisk enters dormancy and emerges with adaptive traits and an increase to all core stats. Cap: +1 per stat per Cocoon Phase, and Phases resolve during Downtime — the death that learns still has to sleep.

abilities.name: Corruptive Reproduction
abilities.effect: Target: 1 consumed gland-bearer · Vector: Contact · Duration: Clock (one Downtime) · Automatic. When a creature with an Augo Gland is consumed by a Cerioisk, a new Cerioisk is produced from the digested remains. It is Curator-run — kin rather than servant — and matures over one Downtime.

subdermin.name: Fractine
subdermin.note: Crystalline beings born from Alteria's mineral-rich geodes fused with SubDermin bio-energies. Bipedal (crystalline quadrupedal brace under duress) · Moderate–Large. Diamond-like, self-repairing crystalline structure. Culture values endurance above all.

abilities.name: Diamond Form
abilities.effect: Self (Reaction) · Duration: 1 round · Automatic · Limit: once per scene. Enter a near-impervious crystalline physical state: resistant to kinetic and elemental damage types, reflecting a portion (1d6) of received damage back at the attacker. While in the state your movement is 0.

abilities.name: Crystal Manipulation
abilities.effect: Target: Objects / Area · Vector: Contact · Duration: Sustained (combat projections) or permanent (built structures) · Automatic. Form barriers, weapons, or structures from surrounding crystal matter; combat projections are temporary, while structures given sufficient uninterrupted time become permanent. Crystal constructs follow object rules — demolition applies.

subdermin.name: Construct
subdermin.note: SubDermin's most catastrophic experiment — condensed life energy housed in composite alloy and Barion matter. Bipedal · Always Large. Endures sun-surface temperatures; survives catastrophic damage; may inherit emotional bleed from consumed source souls. Interitus Enmortem weapons are the sole known counter.

abilities.name: Physical Fracture
abilities.effect: Target: 1 Creature · Vector: Strike · Instant · Damage: equal to the first hit's damage · Resolution: Endurance Check ends the Anchor · Limit: once per round. On a successful physical attack, a second amplified strike triggers automatically, dealing damage equal to the first hit. If the second strike's total exceeds the target's combined defensive and offensive power, the target becomes Anchored — forced prone, unable to reposition until passing an Endurance Check.

abilities.name: Pressuring Advance
abilities.effect: Target: Multi · Vector: Wave · Instant · Area: Radius 50 ft of the destination point · Resolution: Balance Check and Endurance Save · Limit: once per scene. Bonus Action: move at half speed in a burst. All creatures within 50 feet of the destination point must make both checks or suffer stagger (Staggered, pushed 10 ft).

inderi.name: AI'N
inderi.note: Beings of pure mental and spiritual data, born from the Implosion of the First Universe and given form under Protocol Evangeline. No fixed form — inhabit machinery, data streams, and minds; manifested form is Small. Forcibly integrated into vessels (T-BM001) by Indrah; documentation expunged; believed extinct.

abilities.name: Dilation
abilities.effect: Self (Reaction) · Instant · Automatic · Limit: once per round. On failing an AP Check, slow perceived time for incoming attacks; gain Advantage on the next roll made in response.

abilities.name: Replication
abilities.effect: Target: 1 remembered object, construct, or being · Vector: Gaze · Duration: Clock (once per 2 in-universe hours) · Resolution: the target of replication contests with a Control + Inspiration Skill Check against the AI'N's DC; the process cannot fail on the AI'N's side. Manifest a flawless 1:1 replica of any remembered object, construct, or being — only the target's contest can degrade the copy. Caps: a replicated being is an inert, mindless shell — data has no soul to copy; Ciphers, charges, and DNA-locks never replicate.

seraph.name: Fallen Seraph
seraph.note: Seraph stripped of their Antimatter Wings through exile, sacrifice, or catastrophic conflict — relentless terrestrial combatants. Bipedal (glowing wing-scar tissue) · Moderate (6'2"–7'0"). Divine Scar (recessive): antimatter burns that faintly illuminate under stress. Spatial Acuity (dominant): Seraph spatial awareness remains intact.

abilities.name: Scar Beam
abilities.effect: Target: 1 Creature · Vector: Wave (Line 60 ft) · Instant · Damage: Antimatter equal to (Int + Neuronal Capacity Modifier) · Resolution: Dexterity save, half · Limit: three times per encounter. Fire concentrated antimatter from the severed wing-stumps in a precise 60-ft beam. Requires no additional action when incorporated into a Standard Action attack.

abilities.name: Grounded Insight
abilities.effect: Passive (Self) · Always · Area: 60 ft · Automatic. Permanent Advantage on tactical and terrain rolls within 60 feet. Ground-level environments are always treated as familiar terrain.

seraph.name: Ardent Seraph
seraph.note: Seraph whose continual manipulation of space fractured their own existence — flickering across overlapping coordinates while drawing reality toward them. Bipedal (intermittently multi-positional) · Moderate–Large (apparent size fluctuates). Spatial Bleed (recessive): may awaken elsewhere from unconsciousness. Dimensional Sensitivity (dominant): exceptional awareness of dimensional seams.

abilities.name: Fracture Point
abilities.effect: Self · Duration: until the encounter ends · Automatic · Limit: maximum three active anchors; one teleport per round. Place invisible spatial anchors within 60 feet as a Free Action. Teleport to any active anchor as a Bonus Action.

abilities.name: Reality Pull
abilities.effect: Target: Creatures or Objects within 30 ft · Vector: Gaze · Instant · Resolution: unwilling targets make a Strength Save against the Ardent Seraph's Control DC. Pull creatures or objects within 30 feet directly adjacent. Objects up to Large size move automatically.

seraph.name: Oris
seraph.note: The culmination of the once-Orialia and the new Seraph. Bipedal · Tiny or Colossal. Always surrounded by Stardust — used for mobility, moving with it and flying at half Movement Speed.

abilities.name: Localized Pocket
abilities.effect: At Will — Out of Combat / Reaction / Bonus Action · Vector: Anchor · Duration: Sustained · Automatic · Limit: one Phenomenon stored at a time; stored Phenomena fade at scene's end; you cannot Store an effect whose Synaptic cost exceeds your Control stat. While not in active combat, generate a Localized Pocket Space that any part of your body may enter. Upon entering, choose one: Connect — link the Pocket Space to a second space within 65 feet of you. Store — absorb the effect of a Phenomenon that was targeted at you, holding it within the Pocket Space for later use. Redirect — release a stored Phenomenon outward through any other Localized Pocket Space within 50 feet of you. Store and Redirect may each be triggered as a Reaction or a Bonus Action.

abilities.name: Stardust Resurgence
abilities.effect: Target: 1 Creature · Vector: Strike · Instant · Damage: automatic follow-up attack at +5 damage · Resolution: Automatic when thresholds are met · Limit: once per round. When you make an attack, you may generate Stardust to trigger an automatic follow-up attack dealing +5 damage — only if your Attack Power and Neuronal Capacity are both higher than the target's corresponding values. If your Action Priority and Evasion each exceed the target's by more than 10 points, this may instead be triggered as a Reaction in place of your normal attack action.

seraph.name: Riftwalkers
seraph.note: Seraphs who closely resemble their branch's origins — descendants of a greater plane who venture into the mirror realm and bring forth its unstable energy. Bipedal · Moderate (~5 ft). They passively teleport with every step (no mechanical effect). A rare sight in the light of day.

abilities.name: Portalweave
abilities.effect: Self (Active) · Duration: Special · Automatic · Limit: once per round. Open a controlled, lingering portal and enter a rift into a sub-realm of our own; within it, energy fluctuates so frequently that perception is heightened — as if time stopped in the outside world. You may move up to your max movement; any action or exhausting that movement ends this ability and leaves another portal behind at your exit. While inside, the outside world is truly un-interactable — no attacking out, no scouting through barriers you could not otherwise pass.

abilities.name: Anti-Matter Discharge
abilities.effect: Target: Single / Line · Vector: Wave (Line 50 ft) · Instant · Damage: 2d10 Antimatter · Resolution: Dexterity save, half · Limit: twice per encounter. Any connecting portals can emit energy from the dimension they are from: shoot a beam up to 50 feet from an open portal, or channel it between two connected portals. Can be used after exiting a portal, or while placing one down with Portalweave.

stygians.name: Xeno
stygians.note: Engineered Stygian offshoot for biological warfare — the 'Ghosts of Flesh'. Bipedal (tall, emaciated, serpentine) · Colossal (Lanky). Retractable inner proboscis/tongue extends up to 60 ft; excretes viscous black fluid on contact. Covertly allied with the N-T1 through the Deviants faction.

abilities.name: Mutagenic Absorption
abilities.effect: Target: 1 living creature · Vector: Contact · Duration: Instant · Resolution: Endurance save; extent by Curator ruling · Limit: once per scene per target. Excretes black viscous fluid on contact with living tissue, causing genetic destabilization and unpredictable mutations in the target. Caps: mutations are minor-to-hindering, never lethal, and never permanent without the target's player consenting.

abilities.name: Freakish Nature
abilities.effect: Self (Reaction) · Duration: Scene · Resolution: Adaptation Skill Check vs Curator-set DC. If the tongue/proboscis is severed, make the check: if the result exceeds the DC, the tongue regenerates — into multiple tongues equal to half the check result (cap: maximum 3), each able to hold or strike independently until the scene ends.

stygians.name: Greys
stygians.note: Stygians tempered by the Doalith Dominion into peaceful, psychically enhanced beings. Bipedal humanoid · Moderate (~5 ft). Pale grey skin; large reflective psionic eyes; intelligence far above baseline. Currently in mass psionic dormancy after contact with the Indrah consciousness.

abilities.name: Telepathy
abilities.effect: Target: Single or network · Vector: Gaze · Duration: Sustained · Resolution: Wisdom save (unwilling). Communicate telepathically as a bonus or prepared action (or freely outside combat). Also functions as telekinesis: move objects weighing up to (2 × Neuronal Capacity Modifier + Control stat) — never as a direct attack.

abilities.name: Psionic Mold
abilities.effect: Target: Self or 1 organic form · Vector: Contact · Duration: Scene · Resolution: Automatic (self); Endurance save (others) · Limit: +1 Fatigue per use beyond the first each scene. Psionically reshape organic matter — including your own body: adjust muscle density, alter facial features, seal wounds (ends one Bleeding effect). Versatile for infiltration and healing; extended use is draining.

stygians.name: Annunaki
stygians.note: Near-mythological species of uncertain lineage who walk between realities; only faint Stygian genetic echoes. Bipedal · Moderate–Large (6–10 ft standard; rare outliers 12–15 ft). Head shape chosen at creation grants one additional ability. Melamic energy is not one of the 5 standard Genera.

ability.name: Telepathy
ability.effect: Identical mechanics to the Greys' Telepathy ability — telepathic comms and telekinesis (up to 2 × Neuronal Capacity Modifier + Control stat), never as a direct attack.

ability.name: Precognition
ability.effect: Precognitive vision — Advantage on all AP Rolls made against opponents of lower Neuronal Capacity Modifier.

ability.name: Atomic Manipulation
ability.effect: Reshape matter by touch; usable once per hour (cap: unattended objects, Small or smaller — never creatures or held gear).

abilities.name: Melam Manifestation
abilities.effect: Self (Reaction) · Duration: Instant · Resolution: attacker's Control Skill Check vs the Annunaki's DC · Limit: 1 free use per round; additional uses cost Synaptic Space. Emit a Melamic Energy Field defensive shield when attacked. If the attacker fails the check, the attack warps to a random location within 10 feet of you or them (Curator rolls).

abilities.name: Ni Conjuration
abilities.effect: Target: Area · Vector: Ambient · Duration: Sustained · Damage: the conjured element's standard type dice · Resolution: per conjured effect; scale per Curator discretion · Limit: one element at a time. Psionic command over a single element present in the current environment — shape, hurl, or wall it. Ni commands what exists; it does not create.

stygians.name: Sbeindlaer
stygians.note: Descendants of the one who cast the 'Blight Night'. Bipedal humanoid · Moderate (~5 ft). Two skin layers: a controllable outer black void layer (projectile/chitin, regenerates; usage 25% of max HP; range per tile × 2) and a highly blightful inner dark-grey layer. Unique: DHP is a flat additive on the outer layer, integrated into the main health bar.

abilities.name: Unstable Blightness
abilities.effect: Target: Multi · Vector: Ambient (your shadow) · Duration: Sustained · Damage: decay = (Control + Priority) ÷ 15 per turn, absorbed to regenerate your outer layer · Resolution: Endurance save halves that turn's decay. Those who step into your shadows are rapidly decayed and absorbed to regenerate a portion of your outer layer. Inflicts one blight stack per round of exposure, increasing additively by 1 per consecutive round. Upon reaching 8 stacks the blight ruptures: 3d10 Entropy and Incapacitated 1 round, then stacks reset to 4 (Incapacitated 1 hour of passive time outside combat).

abilities.name: Dream Eater
abilities.effect: Target: 1 sleeping creature · Vector: Ambient (outer layer or mind) · Duration: Sustained · Damage: 1d8 Psychic or 1d10 Spirit per round · Resolution: Wisdom save each round ends the invasion. Invade the consciousness of those asleep: harm their soul/mind at the listed rate, prolong their slumber, or communicate within the dream. Caps: the target visibly dreams in distress — observers can wake them, ending the invasion; a target reduced below half HP by Dream Eater wakes automatically. It wounds; it never kills the sleeping.
